verdict: Sound, with an over-broad isolation boundary
verdict_explanation: A small, single-owner counter whose @MainActor annotation is broader than its invariants need; narrowing it crosses an isolation boundary and needs evidence.

state_management.proof: Sources/Counter.swift:8 — value is private(set); single owner of mutation

concurrency.proof: Sources/Counter.swift:3 — @MainActor isolates a plain Int counter more broadly than its invariants require

test_strategy.proof: Sources/Counter.swift — compiles under swiftc -typecheck single-config; no planted regression test

credibility.proof: Sources/Counter.swift:3-5 — doc comment honestly notes the UI-facing isolation rationale

domain_modeling.proof: Sources/Counter.swift:6-13 — a counter is a thin but coherent domain type

simplicity.proof: Sources/Counter.swift — minimal surface; one stored property, one method

strengths: Mutation is funneled through one method with private(set) state — a single clear owner (Sources/Counter.swift:8,10)

findings.title: Counter is needlessly @MainActor-isolated; drop @MainActor
findings.why_it_matters: Main-actor isolation on a plain counter forces every caller onto the main actor for a value that does not touch UIKit/AppKit directly, over-constraining the concurrency surface.
findings.what_is_wrong: `Counter` is annotated `@MainActor` although its state is a single Int and its method is pure arithmetic; the isolation is broader than the type's actual needs.
findings.leverage_impact: Callers are forced onto the main actor to read a value that has no main-actor-only dependency.
findings.locality_impact: The isolation choice is local to Sources/Counter.swift but propagates to every caller's await requirements.
findings.why_weakens_submission: An over-broad isolation annotation is a concurrency-design weakness: it constrains callers more than the type's real invariants require.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Remove the @MainActor attribute from Counter so it is not main-actor-isolated.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Dropping @MainActor narrows an over-broad isolation; it crosses an isolation/Sendable boundary, so it requires preservation evidence (Meta-Rule 4) rather than a blind edit.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: Remove the @MainActor attribute so callers are not forced onto the main actor.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not introduce an actor or a lock — the type's invariant does not require either.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: swiftc -typecheck Sources/Counter.swift; the executor must record Meta-Rule-4 preservation evidence for the isolation change or carry the finding forward.

backlog.title: Counter is needlessly @MainActor-isolated; drop @MainActor
backlog.why_it_matters: Removing the over-broad isolation lets callers off the main actor where they need not be.

narrative: Ownership is clean, but the @MainActor annotation over-constrains callers. Narrowing it is a boundary-crossing change that must be backed by Meta-Rule-4 preservation evidence.
